body:
Rakah, light of the world and humanity.

I humbly pray to receive your light and your blessing to wield your light in these times of crisis. 

Darkness is attacking the world of Kuri. The shadows are coming to life. We have little recourse against them. 

I feel the only way to truly combat this threat against the good people of the world is to secure and sanctify with the pure, unfiltered light of Rakah. 

I beg to be one of the chosen warriors of light for this war against evil and darkness.
name: Halanaestra
subject: A prayer to Rakah

body:
On wrote:

Hello you three wonderul deputies. I'm following a template from another wiki page that had racial abilities. The Orc wiki is pretty light on info, so I tried to take what I could from that and build it up to some racial abilities. Any other suggestions/comments would be great. I wanted to get at least these 3 done before I made them wiki official:

Racial Abilities
Uncontrolled Rage - The very core feature of the Orc Race. She can run faster, jump higher, and becomes immune to charms and fears. This is an impediment to Shagresh. She has trained long and hard to not fall into this state. (deputy approval needed)
Bloodthirst - When wounded in battle, drawing blood from opponents can temporarily relieve her of pain. (deputy approval needed)
Track and Field - A lifetime in the jungles of the Southlands has given her extraordinary conditioning in most of the lands. She is very susceptible to the cold and in the North (deputy approval needed)

https://www.karchan.org/wiki/Shagresh.html
name: Shagresh
subject: Re: Orc Racial Abilities for Wiki

body:
On wrote:

I agree with all of Ephinie's points, and have one minor addition.
One of my biggest pet peeves in duels, is someone's character stopping in the middle of a FIGHT to talk some shit. In a real fight, there would be no time for that, so I'm not able to suspend my disbelief when I see it happen.
Nerene is BAD about it.
No other notes.
name: Victoria
subject: Re: Frosthaven Fray - Nerene vs. Sabin

body:
On wrote:

I vote for Sabin. He’s ridiculously overpowered, but I feel that Nerene has as much call as anyone does to meet his OP antics, so it didn’t seem unfair in that context. The horses gave her a hilarious advantage, and I’ve always been a sucker for yeti attacks. But in the end, I feel Sabin had a slight edge in the area of creativity.
name: Ephinie
subject: Sabin vs. Nerene Frosthaven Fray

body:
Tork enters the arena of Frosthaven trying to keep his breath calm as he walks out. This is so much more different then training with his brother or father at home or even with Lana. Seeing Sabin standing before him he feels a bit giddy. "It is really you. Mr. Sabin. I have heard so many stories from my father about you and my pawpaw Sabre from the past. I will say this is an honor" He gives a bow. "I am Torko the 2nd but everyone calls me Tork. Son of Thaeloc and grand son of Metal_Sabre and Torko. I read the rules and will allow you to flip the coin. A man of your legend deserves respect."

Sabin smiles to Tork and bows respectfully in return. "It's nice to meet you and get to face you in this official capacity Torko the Second. I look forward to see what your elders have taught you so far."

Standing directly in the middle of the frozen arena Sabin receives a horrible affliction for the duration of his duel: CATCHING ON FIRE.

Tork says [to Sabin] : I guess since pick the conditions I will flip the coun. I call tails.

Standing directly in the middle of the frozen arena Tork receives a horrible affliction for the duration of his duel: CRIPPLING PAIN.

Sabin says [to Tork] : Sounds good, you called it.

Wielding a coin, Sabin flips it, letting it smack against the back of his hand. When he lifts his hand to expose the results, the following is revealed: The coin is clearly HEADS.

You say [to Sabin] : You determine who goes first.

Sabin says [to you] : I will being the match.

You say [to Sabin] : your time starts now.

Sabin tosses the coin to Zaria and give her a quick wink, turns back to Tork, and bows once more. "Luck, kid." And backs off to the other end of the stadium, taking his time to look around him and size up his opponent at the same time. He draws his longbow, and sucks in a slow breath, centering himself and balancing lightly on the balls of his feet, closing his eyes for a few seconds as he blocks away the noise and activity in the stands and focuses on the coming match. His eyes snap open at the same time as he pulls an arrow from his quiver, drawing smoothly and swiftly, aiming suddenly high into the sky, firing up into the air almost perpendicular to the ground. His hand blurs as he quickly snaps off four more shots in rapid succession. No sooner has the last arrow left the bow, that he drops it to the ground and beings to walk towards his opponent, his eyes flashing with power as he draws on the mana of the forests, a green glowing nimbus of power beginning to emanate from his body. He brings his arms up, as if holding a massive weapon, thought nothing is there at first. Suddenly a bright bolt of lightning splits the sky and strikes Sabin, the cacophonous roar of thunder slamming into the spectators instantly following the bolt, and in his hands, the older fighter grips a great sword made of lightning. As Sabin moves suddenly towards Tork, he realizes the folly of conjuring the weapon, as he can see and feel his arms now burning with an angry blaze, which is slowly moving up his forearms and threatens to consume his body. The pain is bad already, and likely to get worse if this duel goes on too long, so the elder ranger needs to end this swiftly. He leaps forward and swings the blade diagonally across his foe's body, sure in the knowledge that the blade with either pierce his flesh, or the electricity, if the younger knight-to-be tries to parry or intercept the attack, will surely cause him considering grief as it courses through him muscles.

Tork ready himself when Sabin announced he will start the duel. He draws his El Cid style blade. It is a little shorter then a long sword and thinner and lighter. Ableto swing it faster then he could a bigger sword like his father. The half elf takes a defensive fencing stance with the sword up ready to parry, but unlike most fencing positions his open hand is closed, after he had put it into his pocket before taking position. Tirk is not distracted by the arrows, from his bed time stories of Sabin he knew of his skill with a bow as being a ranger. If the arrows were not aimed at himself then they were for a different reason. When the lightning strike happens the youngster does briefly wonder if his dad is coming hearing the sound of Thunder. That thought is briefly in hus mind as he is in combat. When Sabin comes down with his slashing attack Tork is able to parry most of the damage away but almost too late as the lighting blade did cut into his thin leather armor near his left shoulder. When the leather splits open it isn't skin under but steel. Tork made a mistake he went steel instead of a copper ingot. With his body being steel makes him drop to a kneeas the electricity courses though his body. From his knees he grits his teeth and after Sabin made that big move might leave himself open. The young blademaster goes low for his attack, going the Ranger's legs to try and cut on the calfs or thighs as he attempts to get back to his feet. Most of his body numb from that first attack.

Sabin watches as the blade and electricity do their work, and realizes that the pain from the flames did indeed force him to overextend his attack. His follow through ended up burying the tip of the sword into the ground, but it might very well have saved him in this case. As Tork's swift light sword sings out in the low cut, the crafty elder is able to use his momentum and the blade's leverage from being stuck in the ground to push himself up and over the attack, somersaulting up and over the half-elf. Fiery pain erupts in his right thigh however, as he feels the sharp blade slice through his leather pants , cutting through the muscle, but luckily not deep enough to hit the artery. As Sabin lands on his feet, he hisses in pain, and you can see he is keeping most of his weight off his right leg. The fiery affliction has progressed to his upper arms now, and is even starting to rise up from the insides of his iron boots, evidence that his feet have caught fire now as well. Growling and gritting his teeth, the woodsman decides it's best to use this to any advantage he can as he releases the lightning blade, which disappears as fast as it was summoned, and brings both flaming arms together in front of himself, his fingers now spread wide open and levelled at Tork. Sabin's eyes shift to match the glowing flames that are consuming his body and flesh, as he draws on memories of the firestorms that have wreaked havoc on forests in the dry seasons, and unleashes a massive sheet of flame at his adversary, seeking to ignite Thaeloc's son in the inferno that races out from him.

Tork even in his numb fingers his body still reacting to the crippling pain of being electrocuted can feel the suddle slice of blade on flesh. As the youngster gets to his feet he can see that Sabin is now favoring one leg over the over. In his mind he start to think about how to push this fight. He puts the steel ingot he had in his hand back in his belt. His arm numb and weak starts to move towards Sabin after he did the summersault over him. Every nerve in his body still sending singles of pain and not wanting to work the son of Thaeloc pushes on. This is a fight, This a duel, we never quit till dead the young bkademaster repeats as a montra in his head. As Sabin starts to summon up the flames that have engulfed his body, Tork suddenly stops as he sees the flames start to erupt and come his way. His eyes are wide as he reaches into his pouch feeling around. If the crowd can hear him quickly mumble where is iot, where is it, as he has to search blindly for what he wants. When he cuts his finger on the object he knows it is the one he needs. Just as the flames start to burn his skin his flesh turns a dark shining black, like a black glass. As soon as the flames end Sabin would be met be a leaping Obsidian covered Tork coming in wild to cleave him head to tail.

Sabin drops his hands as the flames engulf his friend's offspring, waiting to see what would be there when they pass, a burnt husk, an untouched foe, or simply nothing. Perhaps the youth possessed the speed to avoid the rushing storm of fire. His deliberation is cut short as the stone covered body of Tork leaps out from the raging blaze, his sword held high and coming down for a powerful blow to split him in twain. With his reflexes slowed from the damaged muscles in his right leg and the burns on his feet, Sabin is left with one option, and that is to block the savage attack. He lifts his arms up above his head, crossing the leather bracers he wears in an X, meeting the blow with the steel-reinforced armor. As the additional weight of the page's enhanced body presses down on the forestwalker, his leg buckles, and thrown off balance, his arms drop, allowing the blade to slash him across the left shoulder, were the blood instantly sizzles and burns as the never-ceasing flames continue their work to envelope Sabin's body. As he falls, the master martial artist kicks his good leg out from under himself, tucks his body and neatly manages to back roll away from Tork, coming up on his left knee. His visage is set in a grimace as he lifts his right arm up to the sky, clenches his fist and pulls his arms down to point at Tork. High whistling noises can be heard as the arrows, summoned back by their owner, streak forth from the sky, each one homing down towards the blademaster, seeking to bury themselves into Tork's body. The moment the youth lifts his eyes to deal with the threat from above, Sabin smashes his fist into the ground, sending a shockwave rolling through the stadium floor to off balance Tork as the flight is arriving.

Tork grins a smile much like his father's as he does push back Sabin after his cross arm block. As the arrows streak down from the sky and as Sabin rolls away. There is a spark still driving Tork. Even as his body is on the verge of giving up the pain still running though his body and the mounting effects. His inner mana is wasted he does drive the attack with swips of his sword as Sabin retreats. Torks eyes never leave Sabin he doesn't look to the sky he can hear the singing of a blade in air. Each arrow just missing him. "I am a Blademaster! You don't think I can hear those tiny dagers sing!" His light aglie body able ro move and dogde each arrow as they fall as he advances on Sabin. When Sabin punches the ground to break his balance as the attack was pushed Tork leaps again allowing himself open for a moment for the final shoot. Knowing this is his pawpaw Sabre friend he pulls back just to give another knick to his midsection.

Sabin admires Tork's incredible dexterity despite the abuse of the battle, but grins slightly despite the burning pain as the agile boy leaps into the air with no cover this time. Unfortunately for Tork, he moment he left the ground everything became predictable trajectory and speed. Sabin waits until the last moment before the strike, and moves with the liquid grace of decades of training, his muscles reacting without any real conscious thought as he slips sideways, his body staying just in front of the blade as he pivots on his knee to the right, his bad leg sweeping out behind him, his right arm coming up and lightly grasping the wrist, providing just enough of a pull to throw Tork off balance, giving Sabin the leverage to pull sharply down on the swordman's arm. Letting go at the instant were he could drive his foe's face into the ground, he allows Tork to smoothly roll and recover from the throw. The elder ranger gets to his feet once more to face his opponent, the flames from the arena's affliction finally ceasing before covering his head and face, though blackened flesh remains. "Great fight, Torko the Second. Very well done." He bows slowly and with respect in his eyes.
name: Zaria
subject: Re: Tork vs. Sabin

Greetings All,
For those not on the discord, I wanted to reach our about guild logon messages.
Yes, they are currently broken when you login, due to a change made in the newest release. You can still see the message without the broken code by typing guilddetails, all one word.
Karn plans on fixing the issue in the next release, so hold on tight until then.
If you have any questions about the new release, just reach out to me! I highly suggest typing help afk to see one of the coolest new additions!
-Victoria

body:
I had a phone call from my mother right after I logged in to say hi. Then I forgot I was logged in.
Deps have the ability to go invisible due to a past request that you granted. I forgot to type 'admin visible on' before I logged out last time.
If there's anything you need help with, I'm always available on Discord. Happy to log in for you anytime.

-Nettie/Victoria
name: Victoria
subject: Apologies!

body:
On wrote:

I'm doing okay. I replied to your email when you asked why I was in the hospital. Short version is, I had sepsis and would have died if I hadn't went to the Emergency Room.

I've just been slowly recovering from that. I was in the hospital six days. I'm doing mostly better now, but am trying to change my habits around and get healthier. Scared my wife to death.
name: Victoria
